sourceTitle: Everything you need to know about setting up and managing an organization in Notion

questions.question: What is the first step to gaining organization‑level control in Notion?

options.text: Creating a workspace for each team
options.explanation: Workspaces are managed within an organization, but they don’t give you organization‑level control until the domain is claimed.

options.text: Verifying your company domain
options.explanation: Domain verification is the first step; it unlocks admin console features, SAML SSO, and the ability to consolidate workspaces.

options.text: Enabling Notion AI for all members
options.explanation: Notion AI is a feature you can toggle later; it does not grant admin privileges.

options.text: Adding at least one organization owner
options.explanation: You need an organization owner to manage settings, but you can’t assign one until the domain is verified.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the difference between an organization owner and a workspace owner?

options.text: Organization owners can only manage workspaces they belong to, while workspace owners can manage the entire organization.
options.explanation: The opposite is true: organization owners have super‑admin rights across the whole organization.

options.text: Workspace owners have full control over organization‑wide settings, but cannot manage their own workspace.
options.explanation: Workspace owners manage their own workspace; they do not have organization‑wide authority.

options.text: Organization owners have authority over all organization‑wide settings, even for workspaces they don’t belong to, while workspace owners manage only their specific workspace.
options.explanation: This captures the hierarchy: organization owners are super admins; workspace owners are limited to their workspace.

options.text: Both roles have identical permissions; the titles are purely cosmetic.
options.explanation: The roles have distinct permission scopes as described in the guide.

questions.question: How can you access the admin console to manage organization‑level settings?

options.text: Click the Notion logo in the top‑left corner.
options.explanation: The logo opens the home page, not the admin console.

options.text: Open the workspace switcher and select “Manage organization”.
options.explanation: The guide specifies that “Manage organization” in the workspace switcher opens the admin console.

options.text: Press Ctrl+Shift+O on any workspace page.
options.explanation: There is no keyboard shortcut mentioned for opening the admin console.

options.text: Navigate to Settings → Advanced → Admin Console.
options.explanation: The admin console is not nested under Settings; it’s accessed via the workspace switcher.

questions.question: Which general‑settings recommendation helps prevent users from creating Notion accounts automatically through SSO?

options.text: Turn on “Enable SSO”.
options.explanation: Enabling SSO allows single sign‑on but does not stop automatic account creation.

options.text: Turn off “Automatic account creation”.
options.explanation: Disabling automatic account creation stops new users from generating Notion accounts via SSO.

options.text: Set login method to “Only SAML SSO”.
options.explanation: This forces SAML as the login method but does not affect automatic account creation.

options.text: Enable “Suppress invite emails from SCIM provisioning”.
options.explanation: Suppressing invite emails controls email notifications, not account creation.

questions.question: What happens when you use the “Bulk apply” option after changing a setting at the workspace level?

options.text: The change is applied only to the selected workspace and no other entities.
options.explanation: Bulk apply is specifically for cascading the change to child entities.

options.text: The change is immediately applied to the workspace and all its associated teamspaces, but you can still tweak individual teamspaces later.
options.explanation: The guide explains that bulk changes cascade down to teamspaces and can be fine‑tuned later.

options.text: The change is scheduled to take effect after 24 hours across the organization.
options.explanation: Changes take effect right away; there is no 24‑hour delay mentioned.

options.text: The change overwrites any workspace‑specific overrides, making all settings uniform across the organization.
options.explanation: Bulk apply affects the workspace and its children, not the entire organization, and overrides can still be set per teamspace.

questions.question: Which security setting should you enable to stop members from publishing sites and forms across the entire organization?

options.text: Allow guest invite requests
options.explanation: This setting controls guest invites, not publishing permissions.

options.text: Disable publishing sites and forms
options.explanation: Turning this on blocks publishing at the organization level, as recommended.

options.text: Enable automatic account creation
options.explanation: This setting is unrelated to publishing controls.

options.text: Turn on SAML SSO
options.explanation: SAML SSO handles authentication, not content publishing.

questions.question: Why does Notion only support a single Identity Provider (IDP) per organization when using SAML SSO?

options.text: Because multiple IDPs would cause duplicate user accounts across workspaces.
options.explanation: The guide notes that only one IDP can be used to avoid conflicts and ensure consistent authentication.

options.text: Because Notion does not integrate with any IDP other than Google.
options.explanation: Notion supports many SAML providers; the limitation is on quantity, not brand.

options.text: Because each workspace must have its own separate IDP.
options.explanation: Workspaces share the organization’s IDP; they don’t need individual providers.

options.text: Because using multiple IDPs would disable SCIM provisioning.
options.explanation: SCIM provisioning can still work with a single IDP; the restriction isn’t about SCIM.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a recommended workspace setting according to the guide?

options.text: Turn on “Limit teamspace creation to only workspace owners”.
options.explanation: This is one of the recommended settings.

options.text: Turn on “Save and display page view analytics”.
options.explanation: Enabling page view analytics is recommended.

options.text: Remove all allowed email domains and rely solely on SSO.
options.explanation: Removing email domains to force SSO is recommended.

options.text: Allow members to create new workspaces without any restrictions.
options.explanation: The guide advises controlling workspace creation, not allowing unrestricted creation.

questions.question: What primary benefit does the audit log in the Data & Compliance tab provide to organization owners?

options.text: It automatically fixes security misconfigurations.
options.explanation: The audit log records actions; it does not automatically remediate issues.

options.text: It shows a timeline of who made which changes across workspaces, helping with governance and compliance.
options.explanation: The guide describes the audit log as a timeline of actions for governance.

options.text: It predicts future usage trends based on past activity.
options.explanation: Predictive analytics belong to the Analytics tab, not the audit log.

options.text: It allows members to edit other members’ permissions directly.
options.explanation: The audit log is read‑only; it does not grant editing rights.

questions.question: If an organization wants to let members request to add new people to a workspace but still retain review control, which setting should they enable?

options.text: Allow member invite requests
options.explanation: Enabling this lets members request invites, which can then be reviewed by owners.

options.text: Allow guest invite requests
options.explanation: That setting concerns guest invitations, not adding new members.

options.text: Allow membership requests
options.explanation: The guide recommends turning this off in favor of SSO.

options.text: Enable automatic account creation
options.explanation: Automatic account creation bypasses the request/review workflow.
